questions.question: Explain the difference between variable declaration and initialization with examples.
expectedPoints: Declaration reserves memory without assigning value
Example: int x; (declaration) vs int y = 5; (initialization)
Variables can be declared then initialized later

questions.question: Compare and contrast the integer and floating-point data types in C++, including their typical sizes, ranges, and appropriate use cases.
expectedPoints: Integer types store whole numbers (int, short, long, long long)
Floating-point types store decimal numbers (float, double, long double)
Size comparisons: short (2) < int (4) ≤ long (4-8) < long long (8)
Precision: float (6-7) < double (15-16) < long double (19+) digits
Appropriate use cases for each type
